data.author: welwordion-sys
data.description: A novel AI architecture where traversal of data representation space is the primary computational mechanism. The system explicitly navigates transformations of problem representations until recognisable patterns emerge, rather than treating representation learning as a byproduct of optimisation.

project_info.notes:
## Project Info
Started April 28 2026. Core idea: instead of find the answer, find the representation in which the answer becomes obvious.

global_tags.notes:
## Global Tags
Shared tags used across chunks for cross-referencing.

sources.title: Kolmogorov Complexity
sources.relevance: Shortest description of data — related to compression criterion for library entries

sources.title: Solomonoff Induction
sources.relevance: Searching over programs — related to traversal over rule space

sources.title: Weisfeiler-Leman Graph Isomorphism Algorithm
sources.relevance: Approximate graph similarity — alternative to exact GI for pattern recognition

sources.title: Graph Neural Networks
sources.relevance: Fuzzy pattern matching over graphs without solving GI explicitly

sources.title: Minimum Description Length (MDL) Principle
sources.relevance: Candidate compression criterion for when a subgraph earns library entry

sources.title: Cognitive Chunking Theory
sources.relevance: Compressing repeated patterns into single units — analogous to translator/library mechanism

sources.title: Category Theory
sources.relevance: Structure-preserving transformations — relevant to translation operations

sources.notes:
## Sources
Related existing concepts identified during sessions. Not direct citations — areas to explore.

findings.summary: Most data structures reduce to directed graphs + traversal rules. Arrays, trees, linked lists, state machines, neural nets are all special cases.
Problem difficulty is often a function of representation, not inherent complexity. Math problem solving and genetic algorithm effectiveness both confirm this.
Graph isomorphism is not known to be in P, but approximate similarity (graph kernels, WL algorithm) may suffice for pattern recognition.
Hybrid representation (graph structure + primitive attributes at leaves) addresses bloat concern without requiring purely graph-based encoding.
Rule space bloat is the lesser evil compared to data space bloat — rules are static one-time design cost, data is dynamic runtime cost.
Library entries must be rule-invariant pairs, not subgraphs alone. A subgraph only has diagnostic value relative to the rules that produced it during traversal.
Frequency of occurrence alone does not earn library entry. Entries persist only when occurrence correlates with labels.

findings.notes:
## Findings
Key insights established across sessions 1-6.

questions.question: What defines the topology of representation space? How do you know you are moving toward a simpler or more recognisable encoding without already having a pattern to compare against?
What compression criterion governs when a subgraph earns its own upper-layer node / library entry? Candidates: frequency, MDL, predictive utility.
How does the data system decide which candidate invariants to retain vs discard without an explicit confirmation step? Reinforcement through labelled use is the current answer — needs formalising.
How does the rewrite system evolve itself — same feedback loop, but what is the fitness signal for its own logic?
Should genetic operations (mutation, crossover) be fixed primitives or should they also be representable as graph rewrites and therefore evolvable?
Is exact graph isomorphism needed or is approximate similarity sufficient for the recognition cascade?

open_questions.notes:
## Open Questions
Unresolved questions as of session 6.

data.approach: Theoretical architecture design through iterative conversation. Ideas developed session by session, with corrections applied when misunderstandings identified. Photo recognition used as concrete fitness function / test case.
constraints: Graph isomorphism computational cost is a known concern
Unbounded parameter growth in hierarchy is a known concern

methodology.notes:
## Methodology
Purely theoretical at this stage. Implementation not started.

terms.definition: Reversible graph transformation. Same data viewed in a different representation. No information lost. Can always be undone. Primary operation of the data transformation system.
Irreversible transformation. Information is destroyed or genuinely created. New structure emerges that cannot be walked back. Permitted only in the logic rewriting system.

Shared store of rule-invariant pairs discovered through labelled training. Has two layers: empirical (label-correlated, permanent) and reasoned (logic-derived, requires human approval to unlock). Read by both systems, written only by the data system (empirical layer).
A library entry consisting of a subgraph paired with the rule context that produced it during traversal. A subgraph alone has no diagnostic value — the rule context is required.
Layer 1 of the shared library. Contains only label-correlated entries. Permanent — never overwritten. Serves as ground truth anchor for the whole system.
Layer 2 of the shared library. Contains logic-derived entries. Only unlocked after human approval of capability threshold. Can be audited and diffed against empirical library.
Collapsing subgraphs upward — compressing two layers into one. A perspective change, not terrain restructuring. Governed by the data system.
Expanding subgraphs downward — decomposing one layer into two. Governed by the data system.
Recognition is not one test but a cascade of partial matches climbing layers until a threshold is met. E.g. pixel layer: no pattern; edge layer: partial structure; object layer: isomorphism fires.

definitions.notes:
## Definitions
Core terms established across sessions 1-6.

frameworks.name: Representation Space Traversal Architecture
frameworks.description: A two-system AI architecture where the primary mechanism is explicit traversal of data representation space, rather than treating representation learning as a byproduct of optimisation.

frameworks.name: Data Transformation System
frameworks.description: Travels representation space using reversible graph transformations (translations). Owns and restructures the shared library. Governs layer fusion and splitting. Writes to empirical library only when occurrence matches labels. Cannot innovate — all operations reversible.

frameworks.name: Logic Rewriting System
frameworks.description: Improves the travel logic of the data system. Operates irreversibly on rules (innovation permitted). Evolves itself by the same mechanism using match quality as fitness signal. No write access to either library layer or data.

frameworks.name: Hierarchical Graph Layer Structure
frameworks.description: Multi-layer graph hierarchy where lower layers hold raw structure/primitives and upper layers hold nodes that represent subgraphs from below. Each layer has its own ruleset. Layers can fuse or split. Input layer is immutable. Middle layers can swap rules without propagating effects upward due to reversibility.

frameworks.name: Hybrid Representation Model
frameworks.description: Graph structure serves as the structural backbone; primitive values (numbers, raw data) are stored as node/edge attributes at leaves. Avoids bloating graph structure with primitive encodings while retaining full graph expressiveness for relationships and transformations.
components: Lazy expansion policy (unfold to graph only when needed)

frameworks.name: Human Capability Threshold Gate
frameworks.description: Human evaluates system readiness before unlocking the Reasoned Library. Threshold is NOT delegated to the system. Candidate criteria: accuracy across minimum distinct tasks, generalisation outside training distribution, internal consistency of logic-derived entries validated against labels.

frameworks.notes:
## Frameworks
Main architectural frameworks as of session 6.

relationships.notes: Only the data system writes to the empirical library
Rewrite system improves travel logic, not data directly
Translation is reversible; innovation is irreversible
Fusion is a perspective change — reversible — therefore belongs to data system
Empirical library is permanent anchor; reasoned library validated against it
Library entries are pairs, not standalone subgraphs
GA mechanism applies to rule graphs — mutation and crossover of rule structure

## Relationships
Key relationships between architectural components.

sessions.summary: Session 1: Core idea introduced. Representation space traversal as primary mechanism. Analogy to math problem solving and GAs. Key concerns: discrete graph limits, primitive bloat, GI cost.

Session 2: Hybrid representation decided. Hierarchical layer architecture proposed. Translator as compression function. Unbounded parameter growth identified as core problem. Compression criterion needed.
Session 3: Graph rewriting + genetic algorithms introduced. Misunderstandings flagged by user — self-modifying recursive interpretation was off-base.
open_threads: clarify GA role — corrected in session 4

sessions.summary: Session 4: Two transformation types defined (translation/innovation). Two-system architecture established. Layer fusion/splitting introduced. Open question: which system governs fusion/splitting.
Session 5: Fusion/splitting assigned to data system. Rewrite system feedback loop defined. Library introduced as shared structure. No known solution graphs — invariants discovered from labels.
Session 6: Library corrected to rule-invariant pairs (not standalone subgraphs). Two-layer library established (empirical + reasoned). Human capability threshold gate defined for reasoned layer.

ideas.idea: Use photo recognition as first concrete fitness function / test domain for the system
Genetic algorithm operates on rule graphs — mutation and crossover of rule structure, not just rule values. This is stronger than standard GAs which evolve values within fixed representation.
Grammar over rule space — impose hierarchy or grammar on rules so rule space itself has navigable structure rather than being flat.
